mechanism_narrative: The Fed holding FEDFUNDS at the effective zero lower bound signals maximal monetary accommodation, compressing equity discount rates and risk premia, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month as liquidity is redirected into risk assets.
failure_condition: Rank IC between FEDFUNDS level and forward 20d returns is below 0.03 over any rolling 2-year window, or returns following near-zero FEDFUNDS readings are not significantly positive (p>0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When UNRATE crosses above a recessionary threshold of 7.5%, the labor market deterioration is largely priced in and historically marks proximity to a cyclical trough, so equities exhibit contrarian positive forward returns over the following quarter as markets anticipate eventual recovery.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns conditional on UNRATE>7.5% are not significantly higher than unconditional baseline, or hit rate for positive returns falls below 55% across the 2008-2010 sample.

mechanism_narrative: Stabilization in HOUST (housing starts flattening rather than declining month-over-month) combined with FEDFUNDS held near zero jointly signal that ultra-cheap financing is beginning to arrest the housing collapse, a leading demand-side indicator that predicts a quarterly S&P 500 recovery.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 550-560k units, near historic lows in Dec 2008
regime_hint: post-shock recovery with zero-rate policy support
failure_condition: Joint HOUST-stabilization/FEDFUNDS-near-zero signal fails to produce positive 60d forward returns in more than 50% of triggered instances, or interaction term adds no incremental Rank IC over either single-series model.

mechanism_narrative: A persistent month-over-month decline in INDPRO reflects ongoing industrial contraction that has not yet been fully discounted by equity markets, and continued negative momentum in industrial production predicts continued negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO revised value 90.8357 down sharply from vintage 103.5966
failure_condition: Rank IC between INDPRO 3-month trailing change and forward 20d returns is not negative and significant (|IC|<0.03) over rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp monthly decline in PAYEMS exceeding 500,000 jobs signals an acute demand shock that triggers immediate risk-off repricing, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the following week as markets react to confirmed labor market deterioration.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS revised to 134,079k for Jan 2009 vintage reflecting large monthly job losses
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns following PAYEMS declines >500k are not significantly negative (p>0.05), or hit rate for negative returns falls below 55%.

mechanism_narrative: At the zero lower bound, further nominal declines in FEDFUNDS lose transmission potency because conventional rate-cut easing is exhausted, so the positive equity response to rate cuts documented in normal cycles fails to materialize, producing muted or negative forward returns until unconventional tools are signaled.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.15% as of Jan 2009, effectively at zero lower bound
failure_condition: 20d forward returns during FEDFUNDS<0.25% periods are significantly positive and comparable to normal easing-cycle returns, invalidating the liquidity-trap dampening claim.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained month-over-month decline in RSAFS (retail sales) below a -1% threshold signals eroding consumer demand that has not been fully reflected in earnings expectations, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the following month as analysts revise consumer-sector estimates downward.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS revised down to 329,787 million from vintage 344,649 million
failure_condition: 20d forward returns following RSAFS declines below -1% are not significantly negative, or Rank IC magnitude stays under 0.03 across rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL shows disinflation approaching zero year-over-year alongside FEDFUNDS pinned near zero, markets price rising deflation risk (liquidity trap dynamics where nominal rate cuts cannot offset falling prices), compressing corporate pricing power and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the following quarter.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL revised to 211.398 with decelerating inflation into late 2008
failure_condition: 60d forward returns during joint low-CPI/zero-rate regime are not significantly negative, or the interaction model fails to outperform single-series CPIAUCSL or FEDFUNDS baselines in Rank IC.
